channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Raila Odinga’s Former Staff Struggle with Isolation and Uncertainty Seven Months After His Death
item.description: About seven months since the passing of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, his former office at Capitol Hill, Nairobi, has become unusually quiet. Employees who once worked in a lively environment fi...

item.title: Navigating the Information Crisis in a Complex World
item.description: In an era marked by interlinked global crises, from environmental degradation and political instability to economic inequality and social disconnection, Katharine Viner explores how the information la...

item.title: Second Breathe Pakistan Conference Highlights Urgent Need for Climate Action in Islamabad

item.title: Sudan Accuses UAE and Ethiopia of Drone Attacks on Khartoum Airport
item.description: The Sudanese government has accused the United Arab Emirates and Ethiopia of orchestrating drone attacks on Khartoum International Airport and other regions. According to Sudan's Foreign Ministry, evi...

item.title: Derek Merrin Wins GOP Primary, Will Challenge Marcy Kaptur in Ohio
item.description: In a closely watched Ohio congressional primary, former state Representative Derek Merrin defeated former ICE official Madison Sheahan to secure the Republican nomination for the 9th Congressional Dis...

item.title: Coroner Suspends Inquest Into Death of Chimamanda Adichie’s Son Pending AG Meeting
item.description: The inquest into the death of 21-month-old Master Nkanu Adichie-Esege, son of famous author Chimamanda Adichie and Ivara Esege, has been suspended by the coroner at JIC Taylor Courthouse, Lagos Island...

item.title: Poulin’s First Playoff Goal Secures Overtime Win for Montreal Victoire Against Minnesota Frost
item.description: In a gripping semifinal matchup of the Professional Women’s Hockey League, Marie-Philip Poulin scored her first career PWHL playoff goal at 4:02 of the third overtime period, securing a 1-0 victory fo...

item.title: Health Authorities Monitor Hantavirus Cases in South Africa
item.description: South Africa’s Health Minister, Aaron Motsoaledi, has appealed to the public to remain calm as authorities trace individuals who may have been in contact with confirmed Hantavirus patients. According ...

item.description: Health authorities, led by the World Health Organization (WHO), are currently investigating a concerning outbreak of a suspected hantavirus infection aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. The vessel, whi...

item.title: Daily Horoscope Insights for May 6, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail presents the daily horoscope for May 6, 2026, offering guidance for all zodiac signs. Those celebrating a birthday today are advised to balance ambition with humour, pursuing their ...

item.title: Pakistan Needs Urgent Action for Climate Resilience and Low-Carbon Growth
item.description: As the monsoon season draws near, Pakistan is reminded of the devastating floods of 2022 and 2025, which caused immense human, economic, and infrastructural losses. While early preparedness is essenti...

item.title: Aurat March leaders briefly detained outside Karachi Press Club during press briefing
item.description: KARACHI: Several prominent leaders of Aurat March, including activist and veteran artist Sheema Kermani, were briefly detained by police outside the Karachi Press Club on Wednesday. The detentions occ...

item.title: Photographs by Nemai Ghosh Document Satyajit Ray’s Later Years and Filmmaking Process
item.description: Nemai Ghosh, who first met Satyajit Ray in 1968 during the making of Goopy Gyne Bagha Byne, served as Ray’s official photographer until the filmmaker’s last project, Agantuk, in 1991. Over these decad...

item.title: Tension Emerges Within INDIA Bloc as DMK Criticises Congress for Backing TVK's Vijay in Tamil Nadu
item.description: Following the recent Tamil Nadu assembly elections, political tensions have escalated within the INDIA bloc. The Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), led by MK Stalin, openly criticised its ally, Congress...

item.title: Advance Metals begins first drilling program at Gavilanes silver project in Mexico
item.description: Advance Metals has started its inaugural drilling program at the Gavilanes silver project in Durango, Mexico, with a 4,500-metre diamond drilling campaign. The project, situated in a renowned silver-p...

item.title: South Africa’s municipal crisis demands structural reform, accountability, and sustainable funding
item.description: Rain in South Africa, traditionally associated with renewal, has increasingly become a source of anxiety for many residents due to failing municipal infrastructure. Potholes worsen, water systems coll...

item.title: Trump temporarily halts US ship escort mission in Strait of Hormuz amid Iran talks
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced on May 5, 2026, that the United States would temporarily pause its operation to escort commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, citing significant progress ...

item.title: Pakistan highlights diplomatic gains and regional role after Marka-i-Haq anniversary
item.description: Pakistani leadership has stated that the country’s response during the Marka-i-Haq conflict last year significantly improved its international image and diplomatic standing. Speaking at events marking...
